Document: SBI Equity Hybrid Fund | Section: fund_overview | Source: https://www.sbimf.com/sbimf-scheme-details/sbi-equity-hybrid-fund-5

## Fund Overview
SBI Equity Hybrid Fund aims to provide investors with opportunities for long-term capital appreciation with liquidity of an open-ended scheme by investing in a mix of debt and equity. The fund invests in a diversified portfolio of stocks of high growth companies and balances risk through fixed income investments. It invests minimum 65% in equity and allocates 20-35% to debt and money market instruments.

Document: SBI Equity Hybrid Fund | Section: key_facts | Source: https://www.sbimf.com/sbimf-scheme-details/sbi-equity-hybrid-fund-5

## Key Facts

- Sub-category: Aggressive Hybrid
- Benchmark: CRISIL Hybrid 35+65 - Aggressive Index (Additional Benchmark: BSE Sensex TRI)
- Fund Manager: Mr. R. Srinivasan (Equity), Mr. Rajeev Radhakrishnan (Debt)
- Inception Date: 09 Oct 1995
- AUM (₹ Cr): 76,292.98 (as of 31 Mar 2026)
- NAV (Direct Growth): ₹345.46 (as of 22 Apr 2026)
- Risk Rating: Very High

Document: SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund (Formerly known as SBI Long Term Equity Fund) | Section: fund_overview | Source: https://www.sbimf.com/sbimf-scheme-details/sbi-elss-tax-saver-fund-(formerly-known-as-sbi-long-term-equity-fund)-3

## Fund Overview
SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund is an equity linked savings scheme designed as a tax saving tool under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961. The fund aims to provide investors with long-term capital appreciation by investing in a portfolio of equity shares. It invests a minimum of 80% of its assets in equity and related instruments. Investments are subject to a statutory lock-in period of 3 years.

Document: SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund (Formerly known as SBI Long Term Equity Fund) | Section: key_facts | Source: https://www.sbimf.com/sbimf-scheme-details/sbi-elss-tax-saver-fund-(formerly-known-as-sbi-long-term-equity-fund)-3

- AUM (₹ Cr): 28,441.67 (as of 31 Mar 2026)
- NAV (Direct Growth): ₹470.92 (as of 22 Apr 2026)
- Risk Rating: Very High
- Lock-in Period: 3 years (statutory for ELSS under Section 80C)

## Taxation
To deliver the benefit of investment in a portfolio of equity shares, while offering deduction on such investment made in the scheme under Section 80C of the Income-tax Act, 1961. Investments in this scheme are subject to a statutory lock-in of 3 years from the date of allotment to avail Section 80C benefits.

Document: SBI Midcap Fund | Section: fund_overview | Source: https://www.sbimf.com/sbimf-scheme-details/sbi-midcap-fund-34

## Fund Overview
SBI Midcap Fund aims to provide investors with opportunities for long-term growth in capital by investing predominantly in a well-diversified basket of equity stocks of midcap companies. The fund predominantly invests (minimum 65%) in midcap stocks. SBI Midcap Fund selectively invests the remaining assets in other equities and debt instruments. The fund follows a blend of growth and value style with a bottom-up stock selection strategy.

Document: SBI Midcap Fund | Section: key_facts | Source: https://www.sbimf.com/sbimf-scheme-details/sbi-midcap-fund-34

## Fund Overview
SBI Small Cap Fund aims to provide investors with opportunities for long-term growth in capital by investing predominantly in a well-diversified basket of equity stocks of small cap companies. The fund predominantly invests (minimum 65%) in small cap stocks. SBI Small Cap Fund can also take exposure in other equities and debt instruments.

Document: ELSS Exit Load — Fee Scenario Explainer | Section: what_it_is | Source: https://investor.sebi.gov.in/knowledge-centre/exit-load.html

## What it is
Exit load is a fee charged by mutual funds when investors redeem units before a specified holding period. It is expressed as a percentage of the redemption value and reduces the final payout received by the investor. The purpose of exit load is to discourage short-term trading and ensure investors stay invested for the intended horizon.

Document: ELSS Exit Load — Fee Scenario Explainer | Section: who_s_involved | Source: https://investor.sebi.gov.in/knowledge-centre/exit-load.html

## Who's involved
Investor, AMC (SBI Mutual Fund), SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India — regulator).

Document: ELSS Exit Load — Fee Scenario Explainer | Section: how_it_s_calculated | Source: https://investor.sebi.gov.in/knowledge-centre/exit-load.html

## How it's calculated
- Exit load is typically a fixed percentage of the NAV at which units are redeemed.
- Different mutual funds have different exit load structures depending on the scheme type and holding period.
- In SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund, exit load is NIL.
- However, investments are subject to a mandatory 3-year lock-in period that prevents redemption during that period entirely.
- Because the lock-in period exceeds 1 year, STCG does not apply to ELSS; only LTCG rules apply on redemption after the lock-in.

Document: ELSS Exit Load — Fee Scenario Explainer | Section: worked_example | Source: https://investor.sebi.gov.in/knowledge-centre/exit-load.html

## Worked Example
Investor invests ₹1,50,000 in SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund on 1 April 2023. The investor claims ₹1,50,000 deduction under Section 80C for FY 2023-24. On 2 April 2026 (after mandatory 3-year lock-in), the investment value is ₹2,10,000. Gain = ₹60,000. Since LTCG up to ₹1,25,000 per FY is exempt (as per 2025-26 rules), LTCG tax = ₹0. Exit load charged = ₹0 (NIL for ELSS).

Document: ELSS Exit Load — Fee Scenario Explainer | Section: edge_cases_and_common_misconceptions | Source: https://investor.sebi.gov.in/knowledge-centre/exit-load.html

## Edge cases and common misconceptions
- Can you redeem ELSS before 3 years? No. The units cannot be redeemed during the lock-in period — it is not just a fee, it is a restriction.
- What about SIP installments? Each SIP installment has its OWN 3-year lock-in measured from that installment's purchase date.
- Is exit load the same as lock-in? No. Exit load is a fee; lock-in is a redemption restriction. ELSS has lock-in but no exit load.

Document: ELSS Exit Load — Fee Scenario Explainer | Section: regulatory_references | Source: https://investor.sebi.gov.in/knowledge-centre/exit-load.html
